reason: Cannot refute on the merits; the change is verified-correct and shipping it is justified. (1) The three flag clauses it removes each contradict the shipped template, confirmed by reading the files: the template auto-injects correlation_id/trace_id via structlog processors add_correlation_id/add_trace_context, auto-traces via FastAPIInstrumentor.instrument_app(app) and SQLAlchemyInstrumentor().instrument(engine=engine), and auto-meters+logs every request (incl. request_failed with cid) via ObservabilityMiddleware; the shipped get_items route carries ZERO manual log/span/metric/SLO, so the current prompt's clauses (untraced/unmetered path=high, error-path-missing-correlation-id, missing-per-endpoint-SLO) would flag every repo route. (2) The baseline findings prove the over-flag is real and blocking: good/instrumented-route r1 emits 2 high + r2 1 high (all missing-correlation_id [auto-injected], unguarded-commit [data-integrity's domain]); good/correlation-id-logging emits a high every repeat (missing exc_info / missing session.rollback [data-integrity's domain]) and r1's high is a forbidden speculative 'IF the middleware does not...' finding. (3) No under-flagging risk: the prompt still reserves high for a mutation/business path with NO structured log of its own; the bad/uninstrumented-route archive_item has no log on either path while both good fixtures add _log.info/_log.warning, so the recall discriminator survives the 'tighten' edit (which only strips cross-domain bait and pins expect.json to the structured-logging-absence dimension). (4) No dirty-good / ambiguous-bad concern: both good fixtures are independently verified clean (more instrumented than shipped get_items, all 5 baseline good-findings are below the codebase bar); the bad fixture remains a genuine in-domain defect after tightening. (5) The proposed recall_min 0.50/fp_max 0.10 is explicitly deferred to Phase-3 re-derivation, not locked in now, so no premature threshold loosening masks a regression. Defaulting to refuted would be unsupported here.
